reforms.description: Denmark has introduced favourable depreciation rules for green investment and increased the tax allowance for R&D expenses from 101.5% to 130%, with a DKK 50 million cap on qualifying expenditures. Additionally, plans are in place to further raise public support for research and development by making the R&D tax subsidy incremental, aiming to enhance incentives for innovation, particularly in small companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms are designed to stimulate R&D investment and innovation in the private sector, which is expected to enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been officially implemented as part of Denmark's ongoing efforts to boost R&D investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms significantly enhance existing tax incentives for R&D, which is a notable change but not a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The research and development tax subsidy remains relatively low by international standards. Further increase public support to research and development by raising the R&D tax subsidy and making it incremental.

reforms.description: Denmark plans to increase business R&D support by 0.25 percentage points of GDP by 2025, aiming to close the gap to the OECD average. This includes raising tax allowances for R&D expenses to 130% for incremental R&D expenses, thereby enhancing public support for business R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is expected to stimulate innovation and productivity in the business sector, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been officially announced and is set to be implemented by 2025.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public funding for business R&D, marking a major policy shift.
reforms.source_quote: The government aims to increase business R&D by 0.25 ppt of GDP by 2025 to halve the gap to the OECD average.

reforms.description: Denmark allocated DKK 700 million to develop green research innovation partnerships, enhancing cooperation between firms and universities to foster innovation. Additionally, the government aims to raise incentives for cooperation between universities and businesses by providing targeted funding and revising intellectual property rights.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Fostering collaboration between academia and industry is expected to enhance innovation and commercialization of research, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding allocation and initiatives have been officially implemented as part of Denmark's innovation strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The allocation of funds and the focus on collaboration represent a significant but not transformative change in policy.
reforms.source_quote: Raise incentives for the cooperation between universities and businesses by providing targeted funding, revising intellectual property rights, and harmonising procedures.

reforms.description: A political agreement was reached to strengthen the integration of healthcare services and develop digital technologies to address shortages in healthcare.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Integrating digital technologies in healthcare is expected to improve service delivery and efficiency, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The political agreement has been reached and initiatives are underway to implement the integration of digital technologies in healthcare.
reforms.importance_rationale: The agreement represents a significant step towards improving healthcare services through technology, though it is not a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: A political agreement was reached to strengthen the integration of healthcare services at the local level and quality assurance.

reforms.description: Denmark has prioritized an additional 6 billion DKK towards equity finance and access to capital in the scale-up phase, aiming to enhance support for innovative start-ups and SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing access to capital for start-ups is expected to stimulate innovation and growth in the entrepreneurial sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been officially announced and is set to be implemented in 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: The additional funding represents a significant enhancement of support for start-ups, though it is not a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Denmark prioritised an additional 6 billion DKK towards equity finance and access to capital in the scale-up phase.

reforms.description: Denmark's government has announced an agreement to increase the number of university places available for English-taught master's programmes, allowing for an additional 1,100 students each year from 2024-2028 and 2,500 from 2029.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing access to higher education is expected to enhance the supply of skilled researchers and innovators, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The agreement has been officially announced and is set to be implemented starting in 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of university places represents a significant step towards improving human capital, though it is not a complete overhaul of the education system.
reforms.source_quote: The recently announced agreement to allow universities to augment their enrolment of students in English-taught master’s programmes by 1,100 each year from 2024-2028, and by 2,500 from 2029 is welcome.

reforms.description: Denmark is preparing a 10-year plan to achieve an additional 10,000 equivalent full-time employees in the public sector through automation and digitisation, which aims to improve productivity in the long-term care sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving productivity in the public sector through automation is expected to enhance efficiency and service delivery, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been officially announced and is set to be implemented by 2025.
reforms.importance_rationale: The 10-year plan represents a significant structural change in how public services will be delivered.
reforms.source_quote: Denmark is preparing a 10-year plan to achieve an additional 10,000 equivalent full-time employees in the public sector via automation and digitisation.
